summary: High‑energy Boulder show with scorching China>Rider and explosive Scarlet>Fire
blurb: The band launches into a fiery first set highlighted by China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and tight playing on Looks Like Rain. The second set erupts with Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain, deep jams on Estimated Prophet > He's Gone, and a soaring Birdsong. Jerry’s guitar shines while Brent adds extra spark in the segues.
review: December 9, 1981 at the University of Colorado’s Events Center captured one of the Dead’s most electrified winter performances. The first set builds momentum with a crisp Cold Rain And Snow opening, moves through a playful Friend Of The Devil and Little Red Rooster, then explodes on China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider – a transition reviewers call “scorching” and “sweet rainfall licks.”  Looks Like Rain draws mixed reactions, but Jerry’s emotive phrasing still shines.  The second set is the show’s high‑water mark: Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain launches an explosive jam that carries through Estimated Prophet > He's Gone, while Phil’s bass drops “huge bombs” and Brent’s keyboards inject extra energy into the segue.  A tight The Other One > Stella Blue stretch leads into a heartfelt Good Lovin’ finale, and the encore’s U.S. Blues & (I Can't Get No) Satisfaction close the night on an uplifting note.  Across all recordings the audience feels the band “locked in,” with Jerry delivering soaring solos, Phil’s intense bass lines, Bob’s solid vocals (and occasional humor), Brent’s vibrant keys, and Mickey/Bill driving the rhythmic storms.  The best‑sounding source is the Clugston soundboard tape – clean, minimal static, and a single splice on Scarlet>Fire that doesn’t detract from the overall brilliance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings (Clugston and Wise) are strongly positive, praising the China>Rider and Scarlet>Fire sequences, Jerry’s solos, and overall energy. Four other sources are mixed – noting low guitar balance or a sloppy first set but still highlighting many strong moments. The net sentiment is moderately positive, warranting a +1.2 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clean soundboard capture with minimal static, solid balance, and the only splice occurs on Scarlet>Fire; it presents the show’s high energy and highlights clearly.

key_highlights: Scorching China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue that drives the first set.
Explosive Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain opening the second set, leading into deep jams.
Jerry’s “sweet rainfall” licks and soaring solos throughout, especially on Birdsong and Stella Blue.
Brent Mydland’s keyboard energy adds extra spark in the Scarlet>Fire transition.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers standout solos – sweet rainfall licks on Looks Like Rain/Cold Rain, emotive phrasing on Stella Blue, and a killer Friend Of The Devil; tone is praised as perfect despite occasional low‑guitar balance notes.
band_performance.Phil: Provides intense bass work, especially “huge bombs” during Rider and powerful unique licks that lift the jams in the second set.
band_performance.Bob: Solid vocal contributions with moments of humor (the "HA!" on He's Gone) and a tasty jam leading into drums; overall vocals are strong though occasionally off‑key.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard interplay shines, adding extra energy to the Scarlet>Fire segue and contributing to the highlighted Bob & Brent jam into the drums.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are described as strong and driving, helping power the space/jam sections and maintaining momentum throughout.
band_performance.Billy: Provides a powerful, steady pulse alongside Mickey, delivering the propulsive rhythm that underpins the high‑energy jams.
